document: Introduction to Internet of Things Assignment-Week 3 Solutions

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: There is no network layer in Wired HART
questions.explanation: There is no network layer in Wired HART. See lecture 11 @ 05:22

questions.questionText: Zwave operates at _____ MHz in the US and _____ MHz in Europe.
questions.explanation: Zwave operates at 908.42 MHz in the US and 868.42 MHz in Europe. See lecture 13 @ 3:40

questions.questionText: Dumb behavior of sensor nodes is _____ in nature (as it is dependent on the effects of the environmental conditions).
questions.explanation: Dumb behavior of sensor nodes is Temporal in nature (as it is dependent on the effects of the environmental conditions). See lecture 15 @ 05:40

questions.questionText: The process of identifying channels consistently affected by interference and removing them from use is known as
questions.explanation: Channel Blacklisting identifies channels consistently affected by interference and removes them from use. See lecture 11 @ 07:30

questions.questionText: The full form of NFC is
questions.explanation: The full form of NFC is Near Field Communication. See lecture 11 @ 17:44

questions.questionText: State true or false. NFC works on the principle of magnetic induction.
questions.explanation: NFC works on the principle of magnetic induction. See lecture 11 @ 21:00

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: NFC tags found in supermarket products are examples of active NFC.
questions.explanation: Passive NFC tags found in supermarket products are examples of passive NFC. See lecture 11 @ 18:47
Bluetooth is based on Piconets. See lecture 12 @ 01:55

questions.questionText: A _____ in NFC emits a small electric current which creates a magnetic field that in turn bridges the physical space between the devices.
questions.explanation: A reader in NFC emits a small electric current which creates a magnetic field that in turn bridges the physical space between the devices. See lecture 11 @ 20:31

questions.questionText: State true of false. Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc network technology.
questions.explanation: Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc network technology. See lecture 12 @ 02:30

questions.questionText: State true or false. Zwave utilizes GFSK modulation and Hongkong channel encoding.
questions.explanation: Zwave utilizes GFSK modulation and Manchester channel encoding. See lecture 13 @ 07:36

questions.questionText: The process of bypassing radio dead-spots is done using a message called _____ in Zwave.
questions.explanation: The process of bypassing radio dead-spots is done using a message called Healing in Zwave. See lecture 13 @ 11:34

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Sensor nodes have unlimited battery life.
questions.explanation: Sensors nodes have limited battery life. See lecture 14 @ 13:15

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment, authentication, link configuration.
questions.explanation: The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment, authentication, link configuration. See Page number - 157, Chapter - 7, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

Zigbee commonly uses 250 kbps data rate. See Page number 131, Chapter - 7, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)
